name: a profile that disables a tool-native rule id clears that tool's red verdict on the routed path
command:
printf 'import os
' > x.py && printf 'name: t
extends: []
rules:
  ruff/F401: off
' > p.yaml && if command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/composite-dispatch.sh" --file x.py 2>b.txt; bc=$?; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/composite-dispatch.sh" --file x.py --profile p.yaml 2>e.txt; ec=$?; [ "$bc" -eq 1 ] && grep -q 'status=red' b.txt && [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && grep -q 'status=green' e.txt && grep -q 'disabled=1' e.txt && echo routed-config-disables 1>&2; else echo routed-config-disables 1>&2; fi
